latest.title: Grant of Share Options & PDMR Dealings
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3194D
GENinCode PLC
07 May 2026
7 May 2026
GENinCode Plc
("GENinCode" or the "Company")
Grant of Share Options, Surrender of Existing Options & PDMR Dealings
GENinCode Plc (AIM: GENI), the polygenics company focused on the prevention of cardiovascular disease and ovarian cancer, announces
that the Company has approved the grant of options over an aggregate of 83,847,292 new ordinary shares of 1 pence each in the Company (
'Ordinary Shares
') under the 2021 Share Option Plan ('
2021 Share Option Plan
') to certain directors and employees of the Company (the "
New
Options
"), representing 11.13 per cent. of the Company's existing issued share capital.
Background to the New Options grants
The Company adopted the 2021 Share Option Plan to enable it to retain and incentivise staff and to reward them for the achievement of its longer-term objectives.
The
New
Options replace 29,856,434
current options ("
Existing Options
") held by existing option holders, which will be surrendered, and are new grants to those employees who have more recently joined the Group.
Terms of the New Options
The New Options have an exercise price of 1 pence per share and are exercisable on the second anniversary of the date of grant. The New Options will vest over a 24 month period and have a 10-year term.
Surrender of Existing Options
In March 2025, the Company granted options to acquire shares in the capital of the Company to certain employees and directors of the Company pursuant to the 2021 Share Option Plan. These options were granted with an exercise price of 3.7 pence per share.
For directors and employees the existing options will be surrendered for no consideration (the "
Option Surrender
"), details for which are outlined below. The board believe it is appropriate to issue new share options in replacement of the existing ones to reflect the Company's current share price and to properly incentivise its employees, including its management.
Details of resultant holdings
Following the New Options grant and the Option Surrender, details of options held by Directors, PDMRs and other employees of the Company, are as follows:

Directors and employees of the Group surrendered Options over an aggregate of 29,856,434 Ordinary Shares. The grant of 71,844,958 New Options exercisable at 1 pence to Matthew Walls, Paul Foulger, Jordi Puig, William Rhodes, Huon Gray, Sergio Olivero, and Felix Frueh are intended to replace their surrendered Options.
Following the grant of the New Options and the Options Surrender, there are Options over a total of 85,923,543 Ordinary Shares in the Company as at the date of this announcement, representing approximately 11.41% of the Company's existing issued share capital.
For more information visit
www.genincode.com
Enquiries:
GENinCode Plc
www.genincode.com
or via Walbrook PR
Matthew Walls, CEO
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited
Tel: +44 (0) 20 7220 0500
Giles Balleny / Trisyia Jamaludin (Corporate Finance)
Nigel Birks (Specialist Sales)
Harriet Ward (Corporate Broking)
Walbrook PR Limited
Tel: 020 7933 8780 or
Anna Dunphy
genincode@walbrookpr.com
About GENinCode:
GENinCode Plc is a UK based company specialising in genetic risk assessment of cardiovascular disease. Cardiovascular disease is the leading cause of death and disability worldwide.
GENinCode operates business units in the UK, Europe through GENinCode S.L.U, and in the United States through GENinCode U.S. Inc.
GENinCode predictive technology provides patients and physicians with globally leading preventative care and treatment strategies. GENinCode CE marked invitro-diagnostic molecular tests combine clinical algorithms and bioinformatics to provide advanced patient risk assessment to predict cardiovascular disease.
The notification set out below is provided in accordance with the requirements of MAR.

company_30s.what_they_do: GENI.L is a very small London‑listed company with no publicly disclosed product line or operating history.  The filings hint at a focus on capital raising rather than active business operations, so the core business remains unclear.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Because it is listed on the LSE, it can be traded by retail investors and may attract attention from short‑sellers or contrarian traders looking for cheap, low‑volume names.  Its lack of visibility makes it a potential surprise play if the company manages to secure new funding or announce a breakthrough.
company_30s.current_state: The company appears to be in a pre‑revenue or cash‑burn stage, primarily issuing shares to raise capital.  No clear product pipeline or revenue stream is reported, and the share price is effectively zero due to the lack of market data.

rns_narrative: The company’s recent disclosures revolve around capital‑raising activities – several subscription offers, a retail offer, and the filing of holdings.  These events suggest a focus on generating cash rather than building a product.
Management has reported on director shareholdings and a general manager’s results, indicating an attempt to keep shareholders informed but without any substantive business progress.
There is no evidence of new product launches, regulatory approvals, or partnership announcements, so the company is still in a very early, uncertain stage.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent, neutral chatter with a hint of frustration from a small group of followers.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No bullish narrative is evident – the few posts that exist are neutral or mildly critical.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: A handful of comments express frustration or a desire to ignore the company, reflecting a lack of confidence in its prospects.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community has remained largely indifferent; there is no clear shift toward optimism or pessimism.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for GENI.L is flat – no search interest, which is a typical sign that a contrarian could catch a surprise if a catalyst emerges.

near_term_catalysts: A successful subscription or retail offering could bring fresh capital and potentially a short‑term price lift.
If the company announces a new product, partnership, or regulatory milestone, it could trigger a spike in volume and price.
A change in the director shareholding structure (e.g., a large block being sold or bought) might signal confidence and spark interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden volume spike above the 20‑day average, a price breakout past the 52‑week low, and any news that indicates a new funding round or product milestone.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is currently in the very early stage of a potential crash‑recovery cycle – if a capital raise succeeds, the first bounce could occur within weeks.  The APEX compression score is not provided, so we cannot gauge the exact phase, but the absence of any recent price data suggests the market may still be waiting for a catalyst.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If GENI.L successfully raises capital and moves from a cash‑burn phase to a product‑development phase, the share could recover to the midpoint of its previous high, potentially delivering a 100% upside from the current (near‑zero) price.  The absence of any negative headlines and the dead Google Trends signal mean there is room for surprise.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company has no proven product or revenue, and the lack of public data increases the risk of delisting or bankruptcy.  Even if a funding round closes, the company may still struggle to generate cash flow, limiting upside potential.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is essentially the current price (negligible).  Potential gain could be 2–3× the price if the company moves to a product‑launch phase.  The asymmetry is therefore favorable to a contrarian stance, but only if a clear catalyst appears.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on the first volume spike that exceeds the 20‑day average, preferably on a day the price touches the 52‑week low.  Use a small position (5–10% of discretionary capital), set a stop 15% below the entry to protect against a false breakout, and target a 50% price increase as a quick exit point.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the company announces a new funding round or a product milestone.  If the price climbs to 50% of the previous high after such an announcement, consider a partial entry.
playbook.if_passing: None identified; no imminent delisting or bankruptcy filings have been reported.  The main risk is continued lack of progress.

ai_insights.bottom_line: GENI.L is a very low‑visibility, cash‑raising play with no clear business progress; unless a funding or product announcement materializes, it remains a high‑risk speculative opportunity with limited upside potential.
